Event: Cyclone Gaja — Very Severe Cyclonic Storm, Bay of Bengal
Season: 2018 North Indian Ocean Cyclone Season (6th named storm)
Duration: 13 days total | 9 days as named cyclone
Genesis: Gulf of Thailand (7 Nov) → Andaman Sea → Bay of Bengal
Peak Wind Speed: 130 km/h (70 kt) | Adirampattinam: 165 km/h | Muthupet: 160 km/h
Min Pressure: 976 hPa at peak | 978 hPa at landfall
Storm Surge: 1.6 m at Karaikal Beach (1.0 m forecasted by IMD)
Flood Type: Multi-factor: Storm surge + Cyclone rain + Flat delta + Canal overflow + Dam releases
Flood Depth: 1–2 m in Cauvery delta agricultural fields
Flood Duration: 2–4 days (48–96 hrs) standing water in flat delta terrain
River Discharge: Cauvery ~800–1,200 m³/s above normal | Backwater flooding 10–20 km
Dam Breach?: NO — All dams held (Mettur, Grand Anicut, Lower Anicut, Vaigai)
Grand Anicut: World's oldest dam (2000 yrs) — 12 canals all overflowed simultaneously
Canal Impact: 56,000 ha crops + trees destroyed from canal overflow
Landslides Occurred?: YES — Kodaikanal (4 fatal), Vattavada, Mattuppetty, Adimali
Cause: 170–280 mm/24hr on saturated Western Ghats slopes
Power Line Damage: 500 km | TANGEDCO loss: Rs 10.5 Bn ($120.5 million)
Crops Destroyed: 56,000 ha | Paddy, banana, teak, cashew, lemon
TN Govt Relief: Rs 1,000 crore | Central Govt: Rs 450 crore
NDRF/SDRF Teams: 9 NDRF + 7 SDRF teams | 3,124 volunteers | 368 medical teams
Seismic Params: All earthquake parameters = Not Applicable / Not Happened
Volcanic Params: All volcanic parameters = Not Applicable / Not Happened
Tsunami Params: Not generated — no submarine earthquake or tsunami from Gaja
